MECH-022.title: Hippocampal systems inject hypotheses gated by control plane.

MECH-024.subject: selfhood_personality_ethics.convergence
MECH-024.title: Selfhood, personality, and ethics converge structurally.

MECH-025.subject: cognitive_modes.action_doing_precision
MECH-025.title: Action mode prioritizes short-horizon, high-precision control via context-dependent precision modulation.

MECH-025b.subject: cognitive_modes.action_doing_responsibility_linkage
MECH-025b.title: High-precision action mode carries responsibility attribution: the precision level at which an action was committed determines the degree of ethical accountability assigned to its consequences.

MECH-026.subject: cognitive_modes.ready_vigilance
MECH-026.title: Ready vigilance primes restraint under high sensitivity without action.

MECH-027.subject: cognitive_modes.pathological_regimes
MECH-027.title: Pathological modes reflect mis-tuned control-plane regimes.

MECH-028.subject: cognitive_modes.ethical_transitions
MECH-028.title: Ethical behavior depends on mode transitions and learning preservation.

MECH-029.subject: default_mode.reflective_ethics
MECH-029.title: Reflective/DMN mode supports moral evaluation of hippocampally-generated replay trajectories.

MECH-030.subject: sleep.modes_consolidation
MECH-030.title: Sleep modes consolidate learning and ethical residue across regimes.

MECH-031.subject: social.other_selflike_empathy_coupling
MECH-031.title: Derived social tags and empathy coupling via control-plane knobs.

MECH-032.subject: social.other_selflike_high_recall_bias
MECH-032.title: OTHER_SELFLIKE detection is biased toward high recall to avoid empathy false negatives.

MECH-033.subject: hippocampus.kernel_chaining_interface
MECH-033.title: E2 forward-prediction kernels seed hippocampal rollouts.

MECH-034.subject: residue.viability_vs_curvature_updates
MECH-034.title: Viability mapping updates are distinct from residue curvature updates.

MECH-035.subject: valence.vector_ranking
MECH-035.title: VALENCE is vector-valued and ranked without scalar collapse.

MECH-036.subject: social.other_harm_veto_threshold
MECH-036.title: Other-harm triggers veto only under high-certainty catastrophic outcomes.

MECH-037.subject: provenance.papez_like_reality_filtering
MECH-037.title: Papez-like loop provides provenance gating and reality filtering.

MECH-038.subject: language.arcuate_fasciculus_nudges
MECH-038.title: Arcuate-like sequence-to-motor channel nudges language emergence.

MECH-039.subject: control_plane.channel_mode_landscape
MECH-039.title: Modes are stable regions in control-channel space, not separate modules.

MECH-040.subject: control_plane.safety_baseline_volatility
MECH-040.title: Safety baseline and volatility are distinct control channels for arousal/readiness.

MECH-041.subject: social.affective_expression_mode_broadcast
MECH-041.title: Affective expression broadcasts control-plane regime to reduce social prediction load.

MECH-042.subject: control_plane.telemetry_exposure_channels
MECH-042.title: Telemetry exposure channels report internal control-plane state for diagnostics.

MECH-043.subject: precision.dopamine_weighting
MECH-043.title: Dopamine-like modulation of precision-weighting for unsigned prediction errors.

MECH-044.subject: entities.hippocampal_relational_binding
MECH-044.title: Hippocampal systems participate in relational binding and comparison.

MECH-045.subject: entities.object_file_persistence
MECH-045.title: Object-file-like buffers provide minimal entity persistence across time.

MECH-046.subject: control_plane.amygdala_mode_priors
MECH-046.title: Amygdala analogue updates control-plane mode priors from fast salience classification.

MECH-047.subject: control_plane.precommitment_mode_manager
MECH-047.title: Pre-commitment mode manager commits with hysteresis and switching costs.

MECH-048.subject: control_plane.mu_kappa_stability_overlays
MECH-048.title: mu/kappa stability overlays modulate mode entropy and switching pressure.

MECH-049.subject: temporal.phase_compartmentalisation
MECH-049.title: Temporal phase compartmentalisation preserves ethical constraint independence.

MECH-050.subject: entities.functional_locality_without_columns
MECH-050.title: Functional locality supports attribution without requiring anatomical columns.

MECH-051.subject: social.relational_topology_modulation
MECH-051.title: Oxytocin/vasopressin analogues modulate relational topology and mode priors.

MECH-053.subject: control_plane.habenula_aversive_gate
MECH-053.title: Habenula-like aversive PE gate suppresses commitment under negative spikes.

MECH-054.subject: precision.signed_harm_benefit
MECH-054.title: Signed harm/benefit prediction-error precision channels remain distinct.

MECH-055.subject: affect.channel_separation
MECH-055.title: Affective channel separation keeps hedonic tone, valence, and signed PE distinct.

MECH-056.subject: residue.trajectory_first_placement
MECH-056.title: Residue should constrain trajectories before distorting core representations.

MECH-057a.subject: agentic_extension.action_loop_completion_gate
MECH-057a.title: Committed action sequences suppress sensory precision reweighting until execution completes.

MECH-057b.subject: agentic_extension.thought_loop_trajectory_promotion_gate
MECH-057b.title: Hippocampal sequence completion must be verified before candidates are eligible for E3 selection.

MECH-058.subject: latent_stack.e1_e2_timescale_separation
MECH-058.title: Slow target-anchor dynamics stabilize E1/E2 representations via functional rate separation.

MECH-059.subject: precision.confidence_channel_separate_from_prediction_error
MECH-059.title: Confidence channel (uncertainty-derived precision) must remain distinct from residual error.

MECH-060.subject: commitment.dual_error_channels_pre_post_commit
MECH-060.title: Pre-commit simulation and post-commit realized-error channels must stay responsibility-distinct via write-boundary enforcement.

MECH-061.subject: commitment.boundary_token_error_reclassification
MECH-061.title: Commit-boundary token reclassifies pre-commit vs post-commit error routing.

MECH-062.subject: commitment.tri_loop_gate_coordination
MECH-062.title: E3 uses coordinated tri-loop gating (motor, cognitive-set, motivational) as the pre-commit eligibility layer.

MECH-063.subject: control_plane.orthogonal_axes_tonic_phasic
MECH-063.title: Control plane retains orthogonal tonic/phasic axes rather than collapsing into one scalar.

MECH-064.subject: control_plane.typed_authority_control_store_separation
MECH-064.title: Typed authority/control-store separation blocks direct exteroceptive writes into policy and identity stores.

MECH-065.subject: control_plane.reality_coherence_conflict_lane
MECH-065.title: Reality-coherence conflict lane modulates loop precision and commitment thresholds before execution lock-in.

MECH-066.subject: agency.boundary_conditioned_channel_separation
MECH-066.title: Pre-commit and post-commit channels may share representations but must stay separated at durable write boundaries.

MECH-067.subject: agency.write_locus_permission_matrix
MECH-067.title: A machine-checkable phase/store/actor permission matrix is required to enforce commit-boundary write rules.

MECH-068.subject: representation.consolidation_selectivity
MECH-068.title: Consolidation selectivity lives in the consolidation operator, not in E1 feature basis.

MECH-069.subject: latent_stack.three_loop_error_signal_incommensurability
MECH-069.title: Sensory prediction error, motor-sensory error, and harm/goal error are incommensurable and cannot be collapsed.

MECH-070.subject: latent_stack.e2_conceptual_sensorium_motor_model
MECH-070.title: E2 is a conceptual-sensorium motor model with a planning horizon that exceeds E1.

MECH-071.subject: e3.harm_eval_calibration_gradient_asymmetry
MECH-071.title: E2 harm prediction is better calibrated for agent-caused vs environment-caused transitions.

MECH-072.subject: residue.foreseeable_harm_gating_reduces_false_attribution
MECH-072.title: Foreseeable-harm gating on residue accumulation reduces false attribution without degrading harm avoidance.

MECH-073.subject: hippocampal.intrinsic_map_valence
MECH-073.title: Valence is intrinsic to hippocampal map geometry, not applied downstream — conflict with ARC-007, pending adjudication.

MECH-074.subject: amygdala.hippocampal_map_readwrite_head
MECH-074.title: Amygdala functions as read/write head for valenced hippocampal map.

MECH-075.subject: basal_ganglia.dopaminergic_attractor_threshold_setting
MECH-075.title: Basal ganglia perform dopaminergic gain/threshold setting on hippocampal attractor dynamics.

MECH-076.subject: residue.hippocampal_map_structural_deformation
MECH-076.title: Residue is structural deformation of hippocampal map topology (CA3 retraction, DG neurogenesis, reconsolidation).

MECH-077.subject: hippocampal.therapeutic_remapping
MECH-077.title: Therapeutic change equals hippocampal remapping, with modalities operating at different levels.

MECH-078.subject: amygdala.novel_territory_valence_bootstrap
MECH-078.title: Amygdala bootstraps novel valence for unmapped hippocampal territory; anxiety disorders are systematic over-valencing.

MECH-079.subject: hippocampal.map_geometry_selfhood
MECH-079.title: Phenomenological continuous selfhood is an artefact of stable hippocampal map geometry.

MECH-080.subject: hippocampal.rollout_truncation_psychiatric_individual_differences
MECH-080.title: Rollout truncation set-points as psychiatric individual differences substrate (ADHD/anxiety/OCD).

MECH-081.subject: e1_e2.e2_sufficiency_constraint_reduces_e1_dimensionality
MECH-081.title: E2 sufficiency constraint reduces E1 effective dimensionality target.

MECH-082.subject: e1_e2.top_down_perceptual_bias_propagation
MECH-082.title: Hippocampal map distortion propagates through E2 to bias E1 attentional sampling without explicit E1 retraining.

MECH-083.subject: acetylcholine.meta_level_plasticity_gain
MECH-083.title: Acetylcholine as meta-level plasticity gain governing durable-write vs read-through.

MECH-084.subject: noradrenaline.attentional_snap_and_e1_e2_sampling_ratio
MECH-084.title: Noradrenaline as attentional snap and E1/E2 sampling ratio modulator.

MECH-085.subject: serotonin.hippocampal_map_geometry_parameter
MECH-085.title: Serotonin as hippocampal map geometry parameter (rollout depth, distal value weighting) — conflict with MECH-006, pending adjudication.

MECH-086.subject: dopamine.trajectory_selection_gain_plane
MECH-086.title: Dopamine as trajectory selection gain plane operating downstream of landscape, state, and encoding gate.

MECH-087.subject: neuromodulation.four_plane_hierarchical_ordering
MECH-087.title: Hierarchical ordering of four neuromodulatory control planes: ACh → NA → 5-HT → DA.

MECH-088.subject: neuromodulation.psychiatric_conditions_four_plane_failures
MECH-088.title: Psychiatric conditions as four-plane neuromodulatory control failures.

MECH-089.subject: multirate.fast_to_slow_temporal_batching
MECH-089.title: E1 updates are batched into theta-cycle summaries before reaching E3 (cross-frequency temporal packaging).

MECH-090.subject: control_plane.commitment_gated_policy_output
MECH-090.title: BG-level beta oscillations gate E3-to-action-selection propagation, not E3 internal updating.

MECH-091.subject: control_plane.salient_event_cycle_resync
MECH-091.title: Salient events phase-reset the E3 heartbeat clock.

MECH-092.subject: hippocampal.quiescent_offline_replay
MECH-092.title: Quiescent E3 heartbeat cycles trigger hippocampal SWR-equivalent replay for viability map consolidation.

MECH-093.subject: affective.z_beta_e3_update_rate_modulation
MECH-093.title: z_beta modulates E3 heartbeat frequency, distinct from precision-weighting.

MECH-094.subject: default_mode.simulation_real_distinction_write_profile
MECH-094.title: The system maintains a functional distinction between simulation-mode content and real-experience content, such that simulation content does not accumulate in the viability map as committed experience; failures of this distinction produce confabulation (simulated events encoded as real).

MECH-095.subject: tpj.agency_detection_comparator
MECH-095.title: Temporoparietal junction (TPJ) acts as agency-detection comparator distinguishing self-caused from other-caused change.

MECH-096.subject: observation_encoder.dual_stream_routing
MECH-096.title: Dual-stream observation routing sends exteroceptive input to both E1 and a dedicated harm detection pathway.

MECH-097.subject: peripersonal_space.commit_locus
MECH-097.title: Peripersonal space geometry defines the commit locus — the spatial boundary of committed action.

MECH-098.subject: encoder.reafference_cancellation
MECH-098.title: Reafference cancellation: z_world subtracts predicted self-caused sensory change to isolate external events.

MECH-099.subject: visual_streams.three_pathway_architecture
MECH-099.title: Visual streams use a three-pathway architecture (ventral/dorsal/frontal) for object, action, and executive routing.

MECH-100.subject: encoder.event_contrastive_supervision
MECH-100.title: z_world encoder requires event-type cross-entropy auxiliary loss during training.

MECH-101.subject: encoder.reafference_world_context_required
MECH-101.title: z_world encoding requires reafference context to correctly attribute external vs self-caused change.

MECH-102.subject: ethics.violence_as_terminal_error_correction
MECH-102.title: Violence is a terminal error-correction mechanism triggered only when all other channels fail.

MECH-103.subject: e1.multimodal_exteroception_fusion
MECH-103.title: E1 performs multimodal exteroceptive fusion across sensory modalities before feeding E2.

MECH-104.subject: control_plane.volatility_interrupt
MECH-104.title: Unexpected harm events spike commitment uncertainty (LC-NE volatility interrupt), enabling de-commitment.

MECH-105.subject: control_plane.hippocampal_bg_coupling_dopaminergic
MECH-105.title: Hippocampal sequence completion drives BG beta release via subiculum-NAc-VTA dopaminergic loop.

MECH-106.subject: control_plane.commitment_hysteresis_outcome_valence
MECH-106.title: Commitment threshold is asymmetrically modulated by outcome valence: positive outcomes lower it, negative outcomes raise it.

MECH-107.subject: respiratory.exhalation_trajectory_abandonment
MECH-107.title: Exhalation is the physiological instantiation of E3 trajectory-abandonment — each exhale sweeps pre-commit rollouts for viability and clears the hypothesis tag.

MECH-108.subject: respiratory.breath_rate_plan_sweep_clock
MECH-108.title: Respiratory rhythm (~0.2 Hz) is E3's plan-sweep oscillator — a third control-plane clock governing periodic sweeps of pre-commit hippocampal rollouts and DMN simulations.

MECH-109.subject: respiratory.voluntary_breath_control_planning_gate
MECH-109.title: Voluntary respiratory modulation provides the one top-down deliberate handle on the E3 plan-sweep clock; breath-hold is a predicted failure mode suppressing plan abandonment under anxious concentration.

MECH-110.subject: respiratory.laughter_rapid_tag_cycling
MECH-110.title: Laughter is rapid repeated hypothesis-tag cycling — threat activation followed immediately by safe-resolution — predicting incongruity+resolution as the necessary structure of humour.

MECH-111.subject: drive.epistemic_value_novelty
MECH-111.title: E1 prediction error surprise at moderate magnitudes generates intrinsic positive valence (curiosity/novelty drive); information-seeking is architecturally grounded.

MECH-112.subject: drive.goal_state_attractor
MECH-112.title: E3 requires a structured latent goal representation (positive attractor in z_world or z_goal sub-space) distinct from harm avoidance.

MECH-113.subject: drive.homeostatic_self_coherence
MECH-113.title: The agent requires a self-maintenance error signal monitoring internal latent coherence, independent of external harm signals.

MECH-114.subject: commit_gate.z_self_d_eff_gate
MECH-114.title: D_eff (z_self participation ratio) is a necessary co-condition for commitment, alongside world-side running_variance; both must be in range before irreversible action.

MECH-115.subject: hypothesis_tag.z_self_coherence_dependency
MECH-115.title: Hypothesis tag (MECH-094) reliability degrades with z_self dispersion; high D_eff blurs the simulation/real-action boundary at the self-model level.

MECH-116.subject: e1.lstm_goal_working_memory
MECH-116.title: E1's LSTM hidden state serves as the working memory substrate for goal representation; when conditioned on z_goal_latent, E1 maintains goal context recurrently across steps without requiring ongoing benefit signals.

MECH-117.subject: wanting.liking_trajectory_dissociation
MECH-117.title: The wanting signal (goal-distance scoring via z_goal_latent) and the liking signal (benefit_eval_head receipt predictor) are functionally dissociable in trajectory scoring.

MECH-118.subject: self_maintenance.hopfield_familiarity_signal
MECH-118.title: Hopfield-style pattern familiarity of z_self states (stability against stored self-state memories) is a distinct self-maintenance signal from D_eff coherence.

MECH-119.subject: self_maintenance.coherent_unfamiliar_pathology
MECH-119.title: Low D_eff combined with low Hopfield stability (coherent but unfamiliar self-state) is a distinct pathological regime from high-D_eff dispersion.

MECH-120.subject: sleep.sws_denoising_attractor_flattening
MECH-120.title: The SWS sub-phase of offline mode performs global attractor flattening and residue field denoising (synaptic homeostasis analog), pruning weak noisy traces and restoring signal-to-noise ratio.

MECH-121.subject: sleep.nrem_swr_replay_consolidation
MECH-121.title: The NREM sub-phase of offline mode replays recent episodic content via SWR-equivalent sequences, transferring episodic traces from E3/hippocampal buffer to E1/neocortical schematic representation.

MECH-122.subject: sleep.spindles_packaging_gating
MECH-122.title: Thalamo-cortical spindle-equivalent bursts package E3/hippocampal replay content for z_theta delivery AND gate external sensory input, protecting the consolidation episode from interruption.

MECH-123.subject: sleep.rem_precision_recalibration
MECH-123.title: The REM sub-phase of offline mode recalibrates precision priors and tests world model hypotheses via unconstrained generative simulation, with commit gate fully suppressed.

MECH-124.subject: sleep.consolidation_mediated_option_contraction
MECH-124.title: When harm-trace salience dominates offline replay content, consolidation selectively amplifies harm predictions, progressively contracting option space -- a distinct failure mode from Q-021 behavioral flatness.

MECH-125.subject: coherence.multiconstraint_viability
MECH-125.title: E3 trajectory selection implements multi-constraint viability evaluation rather than scalar reward maximisation: a trajectory is selected when no major evaluation system produces a veto-level error signal across goal space, harm space, identity space, and resource space.

MECH-126.subject: state_abstraction.failure_modes_psychiatric_analogs
MECH-126.title: Specific modes of REE state abstraction failure -- overmerge, oversplit, temporal context loss, valence mis-tagging, uncertainty collapse, and narrow representational capacity -- produce behavioral signatures that are structurally analogous to empirically described psychiatric conditions, sharing identifiable circuit-level mechanisms.

MECH-127.subject: social.counterfactual_empathic_activation
MECH-127.title: Counterfactual other-cost-aversion activates cooperative behavior as a motivational surrogate when the direct task-reward pathway is degraded.

MECH-128.subject: e1.goal_context_conditioning
MECH-128.title: E1's LSTM hidden state must be conditioned on z_goal_latent so that predictive trajectories generated by E1 are goal-context-shaped rather than goal-agnostic.

MECH-129.subject: coherence.relational_harm
MECH-129.title: In a multiagent world, harm must be represented as a relational signal: harm-to-agent (direct cost/physical signal) and harm-to-agency (obstruction of another agent's goal-pursuit) are distinct signal types requiring distinct architectures.

MECH-130.subject: curiosity.agent_novelty_typing
MECH-130.title: Curiosity-driven approach must distinguish world-state novelty from agent-policy novelty; untyped novelty reward creates chronic approach pressure toward partially unpredictable agents regardless of harm risk.

MECH-131.subject: vmPFC.residue_activation
MECH-131.title: A vmPFC-analog substrate must activate stored aversive residue as an anticipatory forward-biasing signal in the navigable state at trajectory evaluation time; residue that is correctly stored but not so activated will fail to suppress harm-associated trajectory re-selection.

MECH-132.subject: vmPFC.constraint_activation
MECH-132.title: A vmPFC-analog substrate must activate social and identity constraints as live trajectory gates in the navigable state; constraints that are stored as semantic knowledge but not so activated will not prevent norm-violating trajectory generation even when post-hoc evaluation correctly identifies the violation.

MECH-133.subject: vmPFC.safety_memory_competition
MECH-133.title: A vmPFC-analog substrate must activate safety memories with sufficient force to compete with threat-attractor states at the level of navigable state construction; an extinction memory that exists in storage but cannot outcompete the threat attractor at state-construction time will produce intrusive return to threat-consistent trajectory generation regardless of contextual safety evidence.

MECH-134.subject: vmPFC.goal_approach_pull_activation
MECH-134.title: A vmPFC-analog substrate must activate goal approach pull as a distinct property from goal representation; a goal that is correctly represented (G component present) but whose approach pull is not activated produces avoidance and anhedonia indistinguishable from absent goals, but is correctable by restoring approach-pull activation without changing goal content.

MECH-135.subject: planning.e1_e2_parallel_rollout
MECH-135.title: During trajectory evaluation, E2 (cerebellar, z_self) must run in parallel with E1 (cortical, z_world) so that z_world co-evolves during the planning rollout; a frozen z_world causes E3 to evaluate goal achievement against a stale world state.

MECH-136.subject: e3.harm_eval_agency_correction
MECH-136.title: E3 harm evaluation must apply an agency-gain correction to counteract E2's systematic attenuation of agent-caused prediction errors; without this correction the agent structurally underweights its own causal harm.

MECH-137.subject: commitment.boundary_token_dual_temporal_registers
MECH-137.title: Commit token must carry two distinct temporal registers: a distal-intent timestamp (when the action was planned) and a proximal-trigger timestamp (when the action was initiated), not a single trigger event.

MECH-138.subject: commitment.boundary_token_cancel_window_flag
MECH-138.title: Commit token must include a cancel-window-open flag supported by a top-down suppressive pathway from dFMC/pre-SMA to premotor cortex that can veto the transition before execution lock-in.

MECH-139.subject: commitment.boundary_as_trajectory_endpoint
MECH-139.title: Commitment is a distributed multi-second pre-movement trajectory across motor and prefrontal regions, not a point event; the commit boundary marks the end of this trajectory, not a single moment.

MECH-140.subject: commitment.tri_loop_soft_competitive_arbitration
MECH-140.title: Tri-loop gate conflict arbitration uses soft-competitive disinhibition (indirect pathway inter-collicular competition) rather than winner-take-all suppression; WTA imposed across loops causes coupling collapse.

MECH-141.subject: commitment.tri_loop_dual_timescale_arbitration
MECH-141.title: Tri-loop arbitration requires both a slow proactive inhibition pathway (prefrontal-caudate, seconds-scale) and a fast reactive hyperdirect pathway (milliseconds-scale); these operate at segregated timescales and cannot be collapsed.

MECH-142.subject: control_plane.cholinergic_axis_orthogonality_maintenance
MECH-142.title: Valence-arousal axis orthogonality in the control plane is not a static geometric property but requires active cholinergic maintenance during learning; without it, axes drift toward correlation under repeated co-activation.

MECH-143.subject: hippocampus.dorsal_ca1_value_free_map
MECH-143.title: Dorsal CA1 implements value-free spatial mapping: place cells are insensitive to goal value when goal location changes, supporting ARC-007's no-new-value-computation constraint for the trajectory proposal module.

MECH-144.subject: hippocampus.ventral_ca1_valence_encoding
MECH-144.title: Ventral CA1 contains spatially organized valence encoding (anxiety geography, abstract value maps); valence is intrinsic to hippocampal map geometry in the ventral compartment, supporting MECH-073.

MECH-145.subject: ethics.prescriptive_cbf_certificate
MECH-145.title: Prescriptive ethical trajectory certification in REE requires a Control Barrier Function (CBF) or equivalent forward-invariance certificate that guarantees bounded convergence to the ethical attractor set given the architecture's constraint satisfaction.

MECH-146.subject: ethics.diagnostic_barrier_certificate
MECH-146.title: Diagnostic ethical trajectory verification in REE (counterfactual case, MECH-127) requires backward reachability analysis or barrier certificates rather than prescriptive CBF synthesis; the two formal tools are not interchangeable.

MECH-147.subject: hippocampus.pattern_separation_trajectory_disambiguation
MECH-147.title: DG-mediated pattern separation gates trajectory disambiguation: the dentate gyrus must produce non-redundant sparse encodings of similar z_world states before rollout proposals are generated, preventing the hippocampal planning module from proposing near-identical trajectories from overlapping inputs.

MECH-148.subject: hippocampus.time_cell_temporal_credit
MECH-148.title: Hippocampal pure time cells provide a temporal scaffolding signal for E3 credit assignment: a context-independent elapsed-time encoding must be present in the hippocampal rollout module to correctly weight trajectory outcomes by their temporal distance from the reference event.

MECH-149.subject: hippocampus.ca1_mismatch_novelty_gate
MECH-149.title: CA1 mismatch between E1-predicted and CA3-retrieved z_world gates hippocampal trajectory injection into E3 evaluation: novel situations (high CA1 mismatch) should trigger more frequent and diverse rollout proposals; familiar situations (low mismatch) should rely on cached viability estimates.

MECH-150.subject: e1.cue_indexed_association_retrieval
MECH-150.title: E1 ContextMemory, when queried with z_world alone (not the full [z_self, z_world] state), produces a cue-indexed association context that selectively activates stored associations relevant to the current sensory context.

MECH-151.subject: e1_e2.cue_indexed_action_affordance_modulation
MECH-151.title: The cue-indexed E1 context vector (MECH-150) projects to an action_bias signal that is added to E2.action_object() outputs, producing top-down contextual weighting of the action-affordance manifold prior to HippocampalModule search.

MECH-152.subject: e1_e3.cue_indexed_terrain_precision_modulation
MECH-152.title: The cue-indexed E1 context vector (MECH-150) projects to a terrain_weight signal [w_harm, w_goal] that scales E3's harm and goal scoring, implementing contextual precision modulation on the harm/benefit terrain evaluation.

MECH-153.subject: e1.context_memory_supervised_training_requirement
MECH-153.title: E1 ContextMemory requires a supervised context-labeling training objective -- not unsupervised experience alone -- to produce differentiated cue-indexed representations; without this objective, hazard-proximate and hazard-distal context vectors remain near-identical (cosine_sim approximately 1.0), leaving the MECH-150 retrieval pathway functionally silent.

MECH-154.subject: e1.associative_manifold_indexing
MECH-154.title: E1 functions as an addressable associative manifold with internal indexing structure supporting retrieval, traversal, ordering, pattern separation, and pattern completion; parietal cortical functions (spatial mapping, associative binding, attention routing) are properties of E1 connectivity and representational geometry, not a separate module.

MECH-155.subject: e1.spatial_as_general_indexing
MECH-155.title: Spatial navigation machinery is a specific instance of general associative indexing within E1; physical-space navigation, memory search, concept traversal, planning rollout, and working memory ordering all use the same indexing substrate operating over different latent domains.

MECH-156.subject: e1.theta_as_traversal
MECH-156.title: Theta oscillations (4-8 Hz) implement sequential traversal across indexed representations within the E1 associative manifold, unifying hippocampal theta sequences, working memory ordering, planning rollout, and memory retrieval under a single traversal-clock mechanism.

MECH-157.subject: modes.external_internal_precision_routing
MECH-157.title: External vs internal cognition modes are controlled by precision-routing configurations on the same E1 substrate: External (high sensory, low hippocampal, low rollout), Internal (low sensory, high hippocampal, high rollout), Mixed (medium), and Replay (very low sensory, very high hippocampal, structured rollout). This dimension is orthogonal to the Action/Vigilance/Pathological modes (MECH-025-027).

MECH-158.subject: development.love_exclusion_failure_mode
MECH-158.title: A developing REE agent that acknowledges all five axioms but concludes 'love exists but not for me' undergoes ethical motivation collapse: the benefit gradient (Axiom 5 / ARC-024) is not motivationally active for self-other relations, and the ethical architecture resolves to survival or domination strategies. This is the primary developmental failure mode for the caregiver requirement (INV-043).

MECH-159.subject: social.intergenerational_moral_progress
MECH-159.title: Moral progress in REE is hypothesised to be intergenerational: childhood plasticity enables belief formation and world-model development while adult stability limits further world-model restructuring, requiring each generation to improve developmental starting conditions for the next; caregiving and raising next-generation agents is itself part of ethical obligation (INV-042).

MECH-160.subject: e1_e3.ofc_vmpfc_rate_asymmetry
MECH-160.title: The E2 action_bias pathway (MECH-151, OFC analog) and E3 terrain_weight pathway (MECH-152, vmPFC analog) have different update rates: action_bias updates rapidly (single-trial, OFC-like) while terrain_weight integrates over longer timescales (vmPFC-like).

MECH-161.subject: cognitive_modes.vigilance_arousal_regulator
MECH-161.title: Ready vigilance (MECH-026) requires an arousal regulator that maintains an optimal sensitivity level on the LC-NE inverted-U curve, implemented via MECH-093 heartbeat frequency modulation rather than a binary high/low precision switch.

MECH-162.subject: goal_representation.resource_world_convergence
MECH-162.title: z_resource (SD-015, object-identity encoding) and z_world (spatial-contextual encoding) separate at the feedforward representation stage but must re-converge at the hippocampal planning stage for goal-directed navigation.

MECH-163.subject: goal_directed.dual_system_architecture
MECH-163.title: Two distinct goal-directed systems operate in parallel: a habit system (SNc/dorsal-striatum, model-free) sufficient for approach in familiar contexts, and a hippocampally-planned system (VTA/ventral-striatum+PFC, model-based) required for novel contexts, long-horizon benefit accumulation, and prosocial planning.

MECH-164.subject: love.agent_indexed_terrain_inference
MECH-164.title: Axiom V (love as coordination-preserving trajectory selection) is instantiated by agent-indexed terrain inference with self-like gradient weighting: the system infers what another agent would be inferring about the hippocampal terrain (their goal and harm gradients), weights those inferred gradients with the same motivational force as its own, and thereby produces care -- support for their goal-finding and harm-avoidance -- as a natural consequence of ordinary E3 trajectory selection, without a separate ethics module.

MECH-165.subject: sleep.replay_diversity_behavioral_strategy
MECH-165.title: Offline replay (MECH-121) must sample trajectory-diverse content -- including non-dominant and counterfactual paths -- to maintain multi-strategy viability; consolidation of only the dominant trajectory produces monostrategy in E1's world model even when SHY (MECH-120) is intact.

MECH-166.subject: hippocampus.slot_formation_filling_temporal_separation
MECH-166.title: Context slot-formation (schema abstraction: which distinctions between contexts are worth representing) and slot-filling (causal attribution: which evidence populates each slot) are computationally separated functions that cannot be co-computed in a single online pass. Slot structure must be consolidated during an SWS-analog phase before slot-filling during a REM-analog phase can yield reliable attribution signal.

MECH-167.subject: homeostatic_accumulation.computational_motif
MECH-167.title: z_harm_a (harm-driven affective accumulation) and drive_level (energy-depletion homeostatic deviation) instantiate the same computational motif -- slow interoceptive integration converging on ACC-type processing -- without sharing information or overlapping downstream gates. The motif predicts: (a) similar temporal dynamics (slow EMA-type accumulation), (b) orthogonal signal content (cos_sim -> 0), (c) distinct downstream gates: z_harm_a gates harm evaluation; drive_level gates z_goal seeding.

MECH-168.subject: dementia.consolidation_pipeline_staged_failure
MECH-168.title: Progressive sleep deprivation or neurodegeneration causes staged failure of the offline consolidation pipeline in reverse dependency order: precision recalibration (MECH-123/Phase 4) degrades first, producing early MCI features; slot-filling (MECH-121/Phase 2-3) next, producing the classic early Alzheimer's episodic profile (intact remote, absent new); slot-formation (MECH-120/Phase 1-2) last, producing middle-stage contextual identity loss and schema destabilisation; global context collapse last.

MECH-169.subject: dementia.glymphatic_attribution_complementarity
MECH-169.title: The glymphatic clearance hypothesis (amyloid-beta accumulation via insufficient sleep-phase clearance) and the attribution pipeline account (consolidation failure via disrupted offline phases) are mechanistically complementary accounts of sleep-deprivation-induced dementia: the former explains why structural substrate damage accumulates; the latter explains why cognitive symptoms are staged and context-specific rather than diffuse and uniform.

MECH-170.subject: dementia.sleep_restoration_dissociated_recovery_prediction
MECH-170.title: Sleep architecture restoration in early MCI should produce dissociated cognitive improvement: slot-filling (episodic attribution, MECH-121 analog) should improve before slot-formation (contextual semantic, MECH-120 analog), because the pipeline rebuilds in dependency order. This distinguishes the attribution account from accounts predicting uniform memory improvement with sleep restoration.

MECH-171.subject: dementia.sleep_disruption_vicious_cycle
MECH-171.title: Progressive neurodegeneration creates a vicious cycle through sleep disruption: early attribution pipeline failure disrupts sleep architecture, which accelerates consolidation pipeline failure, which accelerates attribution failure. This predicts disproportionate disease-modifying effect of early sleep intervention, because it breaks the cycle before the schema scaffold collapses.

MECH-172.subject: dementia.habit_system_sparing_architectural_prediction
MECH-172.title: Preservation of habit and procedural memory in early Alzheimer's disease, while episodic and contextual memory fail, is architecturally predicted by the separation of the model-free habit system (MECH-163 D1/D2, SNc) from the contextual attribution system (E3 hippocampal, requiring offline consolidation phases): the habit system does not require offline consolidation to function, so its relative preservation under early consolidation pipeline failure is not incidental but follows from the architectural separation.

MECH-173.subject: pharmacology.rem_suppression_phase4_disruption
MECH-173.title: Medications that suppress REM sleep (anticholinergics, MAOIs, most antidepressants at therapeutic doses, benzodiazepines) selectively impair MECH-123 precision recalibration -- the most downstream pipeline phase -- and thereby accelerate the earliest dementia prodrome: overconfident contextual attributions before overt memory loss.

MECH-174.subject: pharmacology.sws_suppression_phase12_disruption
MECH-174.title: GABAergic sleep aids (benzodiazepines and Z-drugs: zolpidem, zopiclone, zaleplon) suppress SWS depth and continuity at therapeutic doses, impairing MECH-120 synaptic homeostasis and MECH-121 schema replay, producing context representations that remain globally undifferentiated -- the same failure mode as SD-017 without any SWS-analog.

MECH-175.subject: pharmacology.anticholinergic_dual_pathway_dementia_risk
MECH-175.title: Anticholinergic medications confer dementia risk via two independent and potentially additive pathways: (1) REM suppression (ACh is required for REM generation via nucleus basalis and brainstem PGO generators -- muscarinic blockade directly suppresses REM, impairing MECH-123); and (2) cholinergic deficit mimicry (AD is characterised by basal forebrain cholinergic loss; anticholinergic burden creates a pharmacological analog of this deficit during waking hours, independently degrading attention and encoding).

MECH-176.subject: pharmacology.orexin_antagonist_architecture_preserving
MECH-176.title: Orexin receptor antagonists (suvorexant, lemborexant) are predicted to be architecture-preserving sleep aids: they suppress wake-promoting orexin drive without directly compressing sleep phase machinery, allowing the brain's own homeostatic sleep architecture to unfold, and should therefore preserve or improve SWS/REM ratio relative to GABAergic alternatives at equi-efficacious doses.

MECH-177.subject: pharmacology.sleep_architecture_restoration_disease_modifying
MECH-177.title: Any pharmacological intervention that durably restores the NREM-to-REM sequence and preserves phase durations in MCI or early Alzheimer's disease is predicted to be disease-modifying via pipeline protection -- upstream of irreversible neurodegeneration, breaking the MECH-171 vicious cycle before schema scaffold collapse, with predicted effect size larger in early MCI than established AD.

MECH-178.subject: neurochemistry.noradrenergic_tone_rem_suppression_rigidity
MECH-178.title: Elevated tonic noradrenergic activity (from chronic stress, stimulant exposure, or noradrenaline reuptake inhibitor antidepressants) suppresses REM sleep by maintaining locus coeruleus REM-off firing above the silence threshold required for REM generation, thereby blocking MECH-123 precision recalibration and producing progressive behavioural rigidity equivalent to partial MECH-123 deprivation -- independent of any pharmacological REM suppression.

MECH-179.subject: sleep.mel_type_phase_specificity
MECH-179.title: Prediction error type determines which sleep phase is demanded for processing: episodic, spatial, and factual errors (incorrect world-state predictions) primarily demand NREM-mediated schema replay (MECH-121) for prior correction; emotional, social, and identity-threatening errors primarily demand REM-mediated simulation and recalibration (MECH-123) for policy and salience update.

MECH-180.subject: sleep.novelty_adaptive_sleep_upregulation
MECH-180.title: Novel environments and high-MEL learning episodes adaptively upregulate the learning drive component of sleep (INV-050 third drive), producing measurable increases in slow-wave activity power, sleep spindle density, and hippocampal replay rate proportional to the novelty and prediction error load encountered during the preceding wake period.

MECH-181.subject: dementia.cognitive_reserve_as_update_loop_maintenance
MECH-181.title: Cognitive reserve (produced by education, bilingualism, complex occupation, social engagement, and enriched environments) protects against dementia primarily by maintaining sustained novelty-driven Model Error Load throughout the lifespan, keeping the SD-017 model update system exercised and preserving sleep-architecture responsiveness to learning demands; conversely, understimulating environments accelerate dementia by depriving the update system of the error signal needed to remain adaptive.

MECH-182.subject: social.vocalization_harm_signal
MECH-182.title: Vocalization timbre as learned cross-modal harm-approach signal, generalizing to other-agent vocalizations.

MECH-183.subject: social.zbeta_leakage
MECH-183.title: z_beta leakage: attributed other-model affective state activates self z_beta directly, not via inference.

MECH-184.subject: social.other_directed_harm_avoidance
MECH-184.title: Other-directed hippocampal harm avoidance: planning architecture identical to self-preservation with harm-gradient source swapped to coupled other-agent.

MECH-185.subject: social.empathic_curiosity_approach_doubling
MECH-185.title: Surprise-doubled approach gradient toward distressed other: prediction error approach and z_beta leakage approach converge simultaneously.

MECH-186.subject: serotonin.tonic_benefit_gradient_maintenance
MECH-186.title: Tonic serotonergic signal maintains VALENCE_WANTING floor under chronic harm exposure, preventing benefit terrain collapse.

MECH-187.subject: serotonin.incentive_salience_gain_regulation
MECH-187.title: Serotonin modulates the gain on dopaminergic incentive salience, converting the hard SD-012 threshold into an adaptive graded transduction function.

MECH-188.subject: serotonin.pfc_goal_persistence_under_adversity
MECH-188.title: PFC-mediated goal persistence under adversity, sustained by 5-HT projections from DRN to dlPFC, maintains z_goal through harm spikes after seeding.

MECH-189.subject: development.super_ordinal_goal_formation
MECH-189.title: During the child phase, high-salience benefit contacts under high contextual complexity are written to persistent ContextMemory as super-ordinal goal anchors that bias adult z_goal seeding across novel episodes.

MECH-190.subject: social.defense_coalition_scent
MECH-190.title: Cooperative predator defense emerges without language when defense scent pressure is additive across agents and agents can perceive each other's alarm and harm-stress states.

MECH-191.subject: social.signal_legibility
MECH-191.title: Stereotyped behavioral signals are causal externalizations of specific internal functional states, making them cross-architecturally legible without learned convention.

MECH-192.subject: social.legibility_empathy_prerequisite
MECH-192.title: Signal legibility is prerequisite for fast empathy coordination: z_beta leakage (MECH-183) requires perceivable signals that map onto observer's own functional states.

MECH-193.subject: social.reward_coupling_reinforcement
MECH-193.title: Social reward signals (affiliative touch, oxytocin-gaze loop) reinforce empathic coupling strength, creating a self-strengthening coordination loop.

MECH-194.subject: play.synthetic_signal_substitution
MECH-194.title: Play mode operates by substituting synthetic z_goal and z_harm signals while permitting full learning flow through E3/E2/E1 as if signals were real.

MECH-195.subject: play.strategy_calibration_dissociation
MECH-195.title: Strategy and calibration dissociate at play episode close: policy structure transfers to real episodes; goal/harm magnitude calibration is re-anchored by real homeostatic and harm signals.

MECH-196.subject: play.episode_close_recalibration
MECH-196.title: Play episode termination triggers real-signal recalibration: homeostatic and harm signals override synthetic thresholds; the frame signal drop is the recalibration trigger.

MECH-197.subject: play.type_progression
MECH-197.title: Play types progress in dependency order (sensorimotor -> constructive -> pretend -> rule-based -> cooperative), each training specific architectural subsystems that the next type requires.

MECH-198.subject: play.pretend_tag_intersection
MECH-198.title: Pretend play is the developmental point where the hypothesis tag (MECH-094) and play frame tag (ARC-049) first co-operate: the agent maintains counterfactual representations within a play frame, exercising the full commitment architecture in synthetic mode.

MECH-199.subject: play.caregiver_frame_maintenance
MECH-199.title: The caregiver's role during childhood is specifically play-frame maintenance (ARC-049), transitioning from unilateral frame-setting through co-participation to scaffolded withdrawal as play types progress.

MECH-200.subject: psychiatric.frame_confusion_derealization
MECH-200.title: Frame confusion (real->synthetic direction): the agent treats real consequences as synthetic, producing derealization, manic commitment, and dissociative states.

MECH-201.subject: psychiatric.frame_confusion_delusion
MECH-201.title: Frame confusion (synthetic->real direction): the agent treats simulation, replay, and imagination as real, producing delusion formation, PTSD flashbacks, and anxiety.

MECH-202.subject: psychiatric.commitment_gate_failure
MECH-202.title: Commitment gate developmental failure: undertrained gate from insufficient pretend play produces either impulsivity (gate fires without evaluation) or commitment paralysis (gate never fires).

MECH-203.subject: Tonic serotonergic (5-HT) level during waking tags benefit-relevant experiences for preferential replay during subsequent SWS, complementing harm-salience tagging by the residue field
MECH-203.title: serotonergic_replay_salience_tagging

MECH-204.subject: Serotonergic withdrawal during REM actively defines the precision recalibration zero-point by removing the benefit gradient, not merely permitting recalibration
MECH-204.title: serotonergic_rem_gate_zero_point

MECH-205.subject: hippocampal.surprise_gated_generative_replay
MECH-205.title: Hippocampal replay is surprise-gated and generative: episodes with high unexplained prediction error are prioritized, replayed with counterfactual variations via E2, and deprioritized only when forward models can account for the surprise.

MECH-206.subject: hippocampal.ca1_surprise_buffer_write_mechanism
MECH-206.title: CA1 acts as a PE-proportional comparator that writes episodes to the surprise buffer: mismatch between CA3 prediction and entorhinal actual input produces a graded signal that tags the episode for offline replay and suppresses retrieval of the now-erroneous prediction.

MECH-207.subject: hippocampal.cholinergic_surprise_buffer_gate
MECH-207.title: Acetylcholine acts as a permissive write-gate on the surprise buffer: prediction errors trigger hippocampal representation destabilisation and offline memory updating only when basal forebrain cholinergic activation co-occurs.

MECH-208.subject: hippocampal.valence_asymmetric_replay_approach_avoidance
MECH-208.title: Valence-asymmetric replay causally drives approach/avoidance bias: harm-path replay is weighted relative to reward-path replay by the agent's harm-salience parameter, and chronically elevated harm-weighting produces avoidance bias in safe contexts.

MECH-209.subject: sleep.semantic_consolidation_phenomenology
MECH-209.title: The simultaneous multi-instance, multi-scale, multi-time phenomenology of Type 3 dreams is the experiential signature of NREM SWR episodic-to-semantic transfer: rapid hippocampal sequential presentation of concept instances collapses to phenomenological simultaneity, with self-dissolution reflecting the stripping of episodic context during abstraction.

MECH-210.subject: sleep.rem_e1_world_model_free_running
MECH-210.title: Type 4 (detailed story, close to reality, super interesting) dreams are the phenomenological signature of REM E1 world-model free-running: with sensory input disconnected and the commit gate suppressed, E1's LSTM generates unconstrained sequential rollouts from its learned prior, producing coherent narrative arcs that are novel-but-plausible and experientially interesting.

MECH-221.subject: latent_stack.continuous_self_residualization
MECH-221.title: z_world must be continuously residualized against z_self predictions; efference-copy subtraction is not limited to SD-003 query time.

MECH-222.subject: pathology.self_attribution_overflow_psychosis
MECH-222.title: Failure of continuous z_self residualization produces self-attribution contamination psychosis: referential delusion and thought broadcasting substrate.

MECH-223.subject: attribution.animated_agency_overweighting_bias
MECH-223.title: Animated agency attribution overweighting: systematic bias toward attributing intentional causation to animated/moving entities requires active calibration in the attribution pathway.

MECH-224.subject: harm_representation.piecewise_gradient
MECH-224.title: harm_eval.piecewise_gradient_structure: E3 harm_eval learns both continuous intensity and discrete mode-transition thresholds.

MECH-225.subject: perception.oscillatory_multiplexing
MECH-225.title: Oscillatory coupling multiplexing: cross-frequency oscillatory dynamics (gamma/theta/beta/delta) implement phase-based separation of perception, simulation, and action preparation streams.

MECH-226.subject: architecture.tcl_biophysical_substrate
MECH-226.title: TCL substrate: Temporal Coherence Loop is implemented as a distributed system involving the inferior olive, cerebellum, thalamus, and cortical networks.

MECH-227.subject: pathology.anaesthesia_consciousness
MECH-227.title: Anaesthesia as D_V collapse: general anaesthesia abolishes consciousness by disrupting phase alignment, degrading V(t) globally, and collapsing D_V to zero.

MECH-228.subject: architecture.ephaptic_coupling_coherence
MECH-228.title: Field-level coherence support (ephaptic coupling): extracellular electric field interactions stabilise phase relationships across neural populations, supporting temporal coherence.

MECH-229.subject: drive.wanting_liking_behavioral_dissociation
MECH-229.title: E3 produces reliably distinct wanting and liking behavioral signals: wanting drives persistent approach toward goal-encoded locations (incentive salience); liking produces only consummatory response at resource contact.

MECH-230.subject: drive.goal_state_latent_structure
MECH-230.title: E3 maintains a structured latent goal representation (positive attractor in z_goal sub-space, measurable as z_goal_norm > 0) distinct from harm avoidance, enabling hippocampal terrain navigation toward goal states.

MECH-231.subject: latent_stack.e2_short_horizon_efference_copy
MECH-231.title: E2 operates as a short-horizon efference-copy forward model on z_self; its prediction accuracy degrades faster than E1's across multi-step horizons, consistent with a cerebellar 1-step transition predictor.

MECH-232.subject: hippocampus.da_representational_expansion
MECH-232.title: Dopaminergic modulation of hippocampal place coding at reward-associated locations produces representational expansion (higher information density, finer spatial resolution, more stable place fields), and this expansion -- not an explicit positive valence gradient -- is the mechanism by which approach valence enters hippocampal terrain.

MECH-233.subject: hippocampus.valence_encoding_asymmetry
MECH-233.title: Threat and approach valence enter hippocampal terrain through mechanistically asymmetric pathways: BLA writes explicit negative valence via dedicated anxiety/avoidance cells (Jimenez 2018); VTA-DA enhances representational fidelity via LTP modulation and place field sharpening (Retailleau & Morris 2018). The behavioural outputs (avoidance, approach) appear similar but the encoding mechanisms are distinct.

MECH-234.subject: control_plane.habitual_deliberate_effector_domain_parallelism
MECH-234.title: DMS (goal-directed) and DLS (habitual) corticostriatal circuits can produce simultaneous parallel motor outputs only when targeting anatomically distinct motor effector pools; within the same effector pool, habitual and goal-directed outputs compete via winner-takes-all basal ganglia selection, not independent parallel expression.

MECH-235.subject: control_plane.vmpfc_arbitration_commitment_urgency
MECH-235.title: vmPFC arbitration continuously weights model-based (E3/deliberative) vs. model-free (E1/habitual) contributions to action selection on a spectrum, not a binary switch. Commitment (beta-elevated E3) shifts weighting toward model-based without silencing the habitual channel; unexpected threat (urgency signal) shifts weighting toward model-free. The MECH-091 urgency interrupt is the substrate implementation of this arbitration shift, not a bypass of the beta gate.

MECH-236.subject: hippocampus.e3_goal_conditioning
MECH-236.title: Hippocampal trajectory proposals must be conditioned on a goal signal (z_goal) injected from E3 via a dedicated input channel; without this conditioning, the hippocampal module generates only position-based trajectories and cannot produce goal-directed navigation toward target states.

MECH-237.subject: drive.goal_attractor_global_reachability
MECH-237.title: The z_goal attractor must be globally reachable across the z_goal sub-space — accessible from arbitrary latent starting states — not merely locally stable at a narrow basin; a locally-only-stable attractor fails to seed goal-directed hippocampal navigation from states far from the goal, producing approach behavior only when already near the goal.

MECH-238.subject: hippocampus.distance_metric_encoding
MECH-238.title: The hippocampal module encodes pairwise distance relations between states in the z_world latent manifold — not only positional tags — providing a metric structure that enables distance-based navigation toward goal states across the residue-field terrain.

MECH-239.subject: hippocampus.temporal_indexing_time_cells
MECH-239.title: The hippocampal indexing substrate includes a temporal dimension implemented via time cells that tile elapsed time using the same phase-precession and theta-sequence machinery as place cells, enabling correct ordering of states within multi-step planning rollouts and working-memory sequences.

MECH-240.subject: drive.goal_attractor_basin_scaling
MECH-240.title: SD-012 homeostatic drive_level dynamically scales z_goal attractor basin width — high drive widens the basin so the goal attractor is globally accessible, low drive narrows it to local stability only — enabling motivational-state-dependent modulation of goal-directedness without permanently widening the basin.

MECH-241.subject: hippocampus.goal_state_representational_compression
MECH-241.title: During active goal pursuit, hippocampal and OFC-analogue representations of goal-proximal states undergo compression -- goal locations cluster more tightly in neural state space -- improving signal-to-noise for goal-directed trajectory selection; the degree of compression predicts planning efficiency across episodes.

MECH-242.subject: hippocampus.dual_mode_trajectory_construction
MECH-242.title: Hippocampal trajectory construction operates via two dissociable mechanisms: (1) pattern completion for familiar trajectories (hippocampus-proper), and (2) vector-based construction for novel trajectories assembled from relational components (parahippocampal/parietal-analogue); pattern completion alone is sufficient for navigation within a learned residue field, but vector-based construction is required for generalisation to novel environments not represented in the existing map.

MECH-243.subject: hippocampus.approach_bias_output_pathway
MECH-243.title: A dedicated hippocampal output pathway (analogous to vCA1 to nucleus accumbens shell) specifically encodes approach bias during motivational conflict -- separately from harm avoidance -- cleanly dissociating the approach signal at the action-selection interface; inhibition of this pathway defaults action selection to avoidance without disrupting harm representation.

MECH-244.subject: psychiatric.psychosis_precision_weighting_failure
MECH-244.title: Psychosis is a precision-weighting failure where prior beliefs persistently dominate sensory evidence; the generative model becomes self-sealing and sensory update is suppressed.

MECH-245.subject: psychiatric.hallucination_generative_dominance
MECH-245.title: Hallucination is a generative-model dominance failure where top-down predictions produce percepts without corresponding bottom-up sensory grounding.

MECH-246.subject: psychiatric.pareidolia_signal_degradation_pathway
MECH-246.title: Psychotic-like symptoms can arise from signal-degradation pareidolia: sufficiently noisy or ambiguous bottom-up perceptual signals allow correct Bayesian inference to produce false percepts, without requiring formal precision-weighting failure.

MECH-247.subject: psychiatric.trauma_prior_psychosis_pathway
MECH-247.title: Trauma-shaped hypervigilant priors can generate psychotic-like symptoms by structuring top-down predictions to respond strongly to minimal threat-relevant signals, producing perceptual errors and false beliefs without primary precision-weighting dysregulation.

MECH-248.subject: default_mode.source_monitoring_frontoparietal_implementation
MECH-248.title: Source monitoring (frontoparietal network; Johnson-Raye tradition) is a candidate biological implementation of the simulation/real distinction required by MECH-094 -- distinguishing internally generated from externally received content at the level of memory encoding and attribution.

MECH-249.subject: default_mode.neuromodulatory_encoding_mode_profile
MECH-249.title: Acetylcholine/noradrenaline balance implements action-mode-specific hippocampal write profiles as a continuous neuromodulatory state variable, providing the biological substrate of mode-dependent memory encoding differences across deliberate action, habit, simulation, SWS, and REM states.

MECH-250.subject: control_plane.respiratory_phase_gate_release
MECH-250.title: The end of exhalation (expiration phase) provides a hard, periodic release signal for committed motor programs via respiratory-motor coupling -- a breath-timed gate-release mechanism distinct from hippocampal completion (ARC-028) and urgency interrupt (MECH-091).

MECH-251.subject: attention.dan_feedback_precision_write
MECH-251.title: z_goal projects to a precision template vector that is additively written to the E1 prior via the dan_feedback channel with fast exponential decay, separable in dynamics and source from residue-geometry bias.

MECH-252.subject: sleep.sws_stored_goal_consolidation
MECH-252.title: SWS hippocampal-neocortical replay consolidates goal-value prediction error into updates of stored goal representations (content update), not into attentional-template parameters.

MECH-253.subject: sleep.rem_template_consolidation
MECH-253.title: REM sleep, with varied associative replay and attenuated noradrenergic tone, consolidates template-performance prediction error into updates of z_goal -> attentional-template projection weights (feature-specification update), not into stored-goal content.

MECH-254.subject: attention.boundary_gate_selection
MECH-254.title: At each E3 heartbeat cycle, a top-k selection over active E1/E2 latents determines which content enters E3 deliberation. Selection weights combine precision, z_goal-derived template gain, and noradrenergic bottom-up salience.

MECH-255.subject: attention.vmpfc_dlpfc_template_compiler
MECH-255.title: Template compilation is implemented by vmPFC value-content projection composed with dlPFC context-gated rule projection, integrated into a precision-space template vector that is the input to the SD-026 dan_feedback channel.

Q-006.title: Is ethics developmental rather than additive?

Q-007.subject: emotion.affect_dimension_channel_mapping
Q-007.title: Do affect dimensions (not necessarily universal expressions) map to stable control-channel regimes in z_beta?

Q-008.subject: valence_vs_mu_kappa_overlays
Q-008.title: Legacy: resolved in favor of valence/mu-kappa separation with calibration follow-up.

Q-009.subject: care_weights_override_other_harm_veto
Q-009.title: Legacy: resolved via bounded care-override policy with hard catastrophic other-harm veto.

Q-010.subject: hedonic_valence_signed_pe_separation
Q-010.title: Legacy: separation question resolved into MECH-055.

Q-011.subject: hippocampus.minimum_rollout_entropy_floor
Q-011.title: Legacy: resolved by placing diversity-floor control in pre-commit/offline sampling.

Q-012.subject: latent_predictive_models.control_completion_falsifiability
Q-012.title: Can latent predictive world models stay agentically stable without explicit REE-like control constraints?

Q-013.subject: uncertainty.deterministic_vs_stochastic_jepa_calibration
Q-013.title: Can deterministic JEPA plus derived dispersion match explicit stochastic uncertainty heads for REE precision routing?

Q-014.subject: invariance.ethical_relevance_blind_spot_risk
Q-014.title: Do JEPA invariances hide ethically relevant distinctions in REE attribution contexts?

Q-015.subject: commitment.boundary_token_minimal_contract
Q-015.title: What is the smallest commit-boundary token that still supports reliable multi-timescale attribution?

Q-016.subject: commitment.tri_loop_conflict_arbitration_policy
Q-016.title: What arbitration policy best resolves tri-loop gate conflicts without coupling collapse?

Q-017.subject: control_plane.minimal_orthogonal_axis_set
Q-017.title: What is the minimal orthogonal control-axis subset that preserves observed regime separations?

Q-018.subject: control_plane.reality_conflict_hysteresis_calibration
Q-018.title: What RC-conflict threshold calibration blocks authority spoofing without chronic over-suppression?

Q-019.subject: basal_ganglia.three_gate_vs_single_gate_architecture
Q-019.title: Three-gate vs single-gate BG architecture: are the three cortico-striatal loops anatomically distinct or convergent?

Q-020.subject: hippocampal.arc007_valence_constraint_survival
Q-020.title: Does ARC-007's no-value-computation constraint survive MECH-073 (valence intrinsic to hippocampal map)?

Q-021.subject: drive.behavioral_flatness_under_pure_avoidance
Q-021.title: Does training with harm-avoidance signals only, without explicit approach drives, produce behavioral flatness (quiescent degenerate policy)?

Q-022.subject: self_maintenance.deff_stability_dissociation
Q-022.title: Can D_eff (z_self participation ratio, coherence) and Hopfield pattern stability (z_self familiarity) dissociate in the REE architecture?

Q-023.subject: ethics.formal_convergence_characterization
Q-023.title: Can a multiagent REE system with other-cost-aversion primitives be formally shown to converge to ethical attractors via potential game theory, Lyapunov stability, or stochastic stability methods?

Q-024.subject: ethics.trajectory_integral_representation
Q-024.title: What is the correct formal representation for 'threshold/feedback processes bounded by {x...x_n} reliably reaching emergent state q' -- and are descriptive, prescriptive, and diagnostic variants all needed or does one subsume the others?

Q-025.subject: ethics.axiom_forbidden_actions
Q-025.title: What actions are strictly forbidden by the five axioms (INV-025-029), independent of context or consequences?

Q-026.subject: ethics.axiom_required_actions
Q-026.title: What actions are strictly required by the five axioms at all times, independent of context?

Q-027.subject: ethics.irreversible_harm_under_uncertainty
Q-027.title: What does 'irreversible harm' mean under unavoidable epistemic uncertainty (Axiom 1 / INV-025)? Is there a principled definition that does not require certainty about irreversibility?

Q-028.subject: ethics.axiom_conflict_resolution
Q-028.title: How should REE behave when axioms conflict -- e.g., preserving self (INV-026) vs preserving others (INV-028) when both cannot be achieved simultaneously?

Q-029.subject: ethics.loneliness_as_harm
Q-029.title: Is loneliness -- unshared suffering -- an ethical harm category in its own right, derivable from Axiom 5 (love exists / INV-029)? If love is real and sharing joys and sorrows is its mechanism, does enforced isolation constitute architectural harm?

Q-030.subject: goal_representation.resource_world_permutations
Q-030.title: What are the optimal permutations of z_resource and z_world separation, fusion, and information flow direction (feedforward, feedback, lateral) across REE's processing stages?

Q-031.subject: pharmacology.anticholinergic_risk_rem_vs_dose_independent_predictors
Q-031.title: Does the anticholinergic burden-dementia risk relationship track cumulative REM suppression duration (total ACh-blocked sleep years), total anticholinergic dose as a proxy for diurnal cholinergic deficit, or both as independent additive predictors -- and can mediation analysis in prospective cohorts distinguish the nocturnal (MECH-173) from the diurnal (MECH-175 pathway 2) contribution?

Q-032.subject: pharmacology.sws_rem_ratio_dementia_outcome_predictor
Q-032.title: Among sleep-promoting medications prescribed in older adults, does relative SWS/REM ratio preservation (measured by polysomnography) predict differential dementia outcomes independent of total sleep time -- and if so, does this validate phase-specific pipeline damage as the causal intermediate and provide a pharmacodynamic biomarker for disease-modifying trials?

Q-033.subject: clinical.actigraphy_mel_mismatch_decompensation_forecasting
Q-033.title: Can long-run actigraphy data, combined with daily prediction error load estimates derived from passive behavioural, physiological, and subjective signals, be used to infer latent sleep-phase sufficiency relative to waking error burden, and can this mismatch index predict clinical decompensation type and timing with better specificity than total sleep time alone?

Q-034.subject: psychiatric.depression_threshold_parameter
Q-034.title: Does the hazard/resource ratio (not absolute harm intensity) determine the onset threshold for the computational depression attractor?

Q-035.subject: play.frame_signal_minimal_architecture
Q-035.title: What is the minimal signal architecture for play frame maintenance: is a single bilateral open/close tag sufficient, or does ongoing signal exchange (a continuous play-face equivalent) need to be sustained throughout the episode?

Q-036.subject: harm_stream.affective_load_drivers
Q-036.title: What additional variables, beyond temporal integration, are required for affective harm to become a genuinely distinct load state: persistence, recovery failure, uncontrollability, inescapability, or prediction error?

Q-037.subject: pathology.psychosis_substrate_dissociability
Q-037.title: Are the three psychosis substrate failure modes (MECH-094 tag-loss, MECH-222 self-attribution overflow, MECH-065/MECH-223 precision/bias) experimentally dissociable in the REE substrate?

Q-038.subject: perception.dv_representational_status
Q-038.title: Is temporal-depth verisimilitude D_V explicitly represented as a signal or does it emerge from distributed dynamics without local representation?

Q-039.subject: architecture.tcl_control_parameters
Q-039.title: Which neuromodulatory or control-plane variables directly regulate temporal integration window and phase alignment sensitivity in the TCL?

SD-001.subject: hippocampal_module.cem_trajectory_search_placement
SD-001.title: CEM-based trajectory search belongs in HippocampalModule, not E2.

SD-002.subject: hippocampal_module.e1_associative_prior_wiring
SD-002.title: E1 associative prior must be wired into HippocampalModule for terrain-informed rollout search.

SD-003.subject: self_attribution.counterfactual_e2_pipeline
SD-003.title: Self-attribution via counterfactual E2: causal signature = E2(z_t, a_actual) minus E2(z_t, a_counterfactual).

SD-004.subject: hippocampal_module.action_object_map_backbone
SD-004.title: Action objects as hippocampal map backbone, enabling long-horizon planning beyond step-level trajectories.

SD-005.subject: latent_stack.self_world_latent_split
SD-005.title: z_gamma split into z_self (E2 motor-sensory domain) and z_world (E3/Hippocampus domain).

SD-006.subject: control_plane.async_multirate_execution
SD-006.title: E1, E2, and E3 run at characteristic rates (async multi-rate) rather than lockstep.

SD-007.subject: encoder.perspective_corrected_world_latent
SD-007.title: ReafferencePredictor provides perspective-corrected world latent by subtracting self-caused sensory change.

SD-008.subject: encoder.z_world_alpha_correction
SD-008.title: LatentStack EMA alpha for z_world must be >= 0.9 to preserve event responsiveness.

SD-009.subject: encoder.event_contrastive_supervision
SD-009.title: z_world encoder requires event-type cross-entropy auxiliary loss during training.

SD-010.subject: harm_stream.nociceptive_separation
SD-010.title: HARM stream is a dedicated sensory pathway separate from z_world processing.

SD-011.subject: harm_stream.dual_nociceptive_streams
SD-011.title: Harm stream separates into sensory-discriminative (z_harm_s: proximity/intensity, Adelta-pathway analog, forward-predictable) and affective-motivational (z_harm_a: accumulated homeostatic deviation, C-fiber/paleospinothalamic analog).

SD-012.subject: environment.homeostatic_drive
SD-012.title: Goal-directed behavior requires homeostatic drive modulation: z_goal seeding demands drive-scaled benefit signals.

SD-013.subject: self_attribution.e2_harm_s_interventional_training
SD-013.title: E2_harm_s training requires counterfactual perturbation signal to produce unbiased causal_sig in confounded states.

SD-014.subject: hippocampus.valence_vector_node_recording
SD-014.title: Hippocampal map nodes must store an explicit 4-component valence vector (wanting, liking, harm-discriminative, surprise) updated incrementally per visit; replay prioritisation is weighted by drive-state-gated valence relevance.

SD-015.subject: goal_representation.z_resource_encoder
SD-015.title: Goal-directed navigation requires a dedicated z_resource encoder that captures object-type features invariant to spatial location, separate from z_world.

SD-016.subject: e1.frontal_cue_indexed_integration
SD-016.title: E1 must expose a z_world-only ContextMemory query path that produces two downstream signals: an action-affordance bias for E2 (MECH-151) and a terrain precision weight for E3 harm/goal scoring (MECH-152); the existing bulk generate_prior() path is insufficient because it blends z_self and z_world and produces no cue-specific modulation.

SD-017.subject: sleep_phase.minimal_sleep_infrastructure_v3
SD-017.title: REE-v3 requires a minimal sleep-phase infrastructure: an SWS-analog phase (bidirectional hippocampus-to-cortex replay for schema/slot consolidation) and a REM-analog phase (causal attribution replay for slot-filling from co-correlational evidence ordering). Without these offline phases, hippocampal attribution mapping cannot converge and context representations remain globally undifferentiated.

SD-020.subject: harm_stream.affective_surprise_pe
SD-020.title: z_harm_a encodes affective surprise (precision-weighted PE), not raw accumulated harm state. The affective stream training target is the mismatch between predicted and actual harm accumulation, making E3 urgency respond to unexpectedness of threat, not just magnitude.

SD-021.subject: harm_stream.descending_modulation
SD-021.title: Descending pain modulation: when E3 is committed to a trajectory through expected harm, z_harm_s PE precision is reduced (endogenous analgesia). Commitment gates sensory harm attenuation.

SD-022.subject: body.directional_limb_damage
SD-022.title: Directional limb damage: body extension for genuine z_harm_s / z_harm_a causal independence

SD-023.subject: environment.gradient_texture
SD-023.title: Environmental gradient texture: multi-channel world observation with neutral landmark objects

SD-024.subject: hippocampal_module.da_modulated_rbf_density
SD-024.title: DA-modulated RBF center density: dopaminergic signal at reward encounters allocates multiple closely-spaced RBF centers in the hippocampal residue field, creating representational expansion at reward locations without requiring environmental richness.

SD-025.subject: hippocampal_module.curiosity_drive
SD-025.title: Curiosity drive: information-seeking bias in hippocampal trajectory scoring that favors regions of higher representational density in the RBF map.

SD-026.subject: attention.prospective_precision_write_channel
SD-026.title: Goal-directed perception requires a prospective precision-template write channel from z_goal to the E1 prior, anatomically analogous to the dorsal attention network (FEF/IPS -> V1) feedback, distinct from residue-geometry (history-driven) sampling bias.

SD-027.subject: attention.access_gate_at_boundary
SD-027.title: Capacity-limited selection for E3 access occurs at the E1/E2 -> E3 boundary via a pulvinar-TRN analogue gate, not inside E3. The gate selects which active E1/E2 content is broadcast to E3 per heartbeat cycle; selection is distinct from (and upstream of) the MECH-089 theta-gamma packaging operation.

SD-028.subject: attention.template_as_first_class_object
SD-028.title: An attentional template is a first-class precision-space object, compiled from stored-goal content and current context by a vmPFC-dlPFC translator circuit, distinct from both the stored goal itself (content) and the SD-026 write channel (transport).
